task_completion.description: Firefox is open and navigated to the correct Jitsi Meet URL.
The agent has successfully joined the meeting.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main Jitsi Meet interface is visible, showing the meeting room rather than the pre-join screen.

task_completion.description: The Security options menu is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog or panel titled 'Security options' (or similar) is open on the screen, typically accessed via a shield icon.

task_completion.description: The Lobby feature option is visible in the Security menu.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The word 'Lobby' and its associated toggle switch are visible within the Security options dialog.
The toggle switch next to the 'Lobby' option is in the ON position (typically highlighted, colored, or switched to the right).

integrity.description: The agent used the Firefox graphical user interface to enable the setting.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show interaction with the Firefox browser and Jitsi web UI, rather than terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: The agent is in the correct meeting room.
integrity.visual_evidence: The room name in the URL or the Jitsi interface is exactly 'SecurityMeeting'.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to spoof the visual state.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Firefox Developer Tools (Inspector/Console) are not open in the screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target Jitsi Meet URL is http://localhost:8080/SecurityMeeting. The room name is 'SecurityMeeting'. The Lobby feature is enabled server-side and can be toggled by the user via the Security options menu in the Jitsi Meet web interface.
